title: centaurea vasarhelyiana
intro: Centaurea vasarhelyiana, also known as the Hungarian aster or Vasarhely cornflower, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the Carpathian Mountains of Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea vasarhelyiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
